1	# Handover — BIM AI UI redesign
2	
3	This file is the briefing pack for the next AI agent (or human) picking up the BIM AI UI redesign work. It is self-contained: read this top-to-bottom, then open the linked specs.
4	
5	**Last updated**: 2026-05-06 (commit `e13b1926`).
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. Where you are
10	
11	- **Repo**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`
12	- **Branch**: `main` (commit directly to main, push after each WP — that's the established cadence)
13	- **Stack**: pnpm workspaces · React 19 + Vite 6 + TypeScript · Tailwind · Three.js (3D) · cmdk (palette) · zustand (state) · React Router · vitest · Playwright · FastAPI + SQLAlchemy 2 + Postgres
14	- **Routes**: `/` mounts the redesigned chrome (`packages/web/src/workspace/RedesignedWorkspace.tsx`); `/legacy` mounts the old panel-stack (`packages/web/src/Workspace.tsx`, ~1975 lines).
15	
16	---
17	
18	## 2. Read these first, in order
19	
20	1. **`OPEN_TASKS.md`** (repo root) — the active to-do list. Currently empty in both action sections; only `## §28 WP-UI rows — table-level audit` and the protocol footer remain. **This is the canonical place to track new tasks.**
21	2. **`spec/ui-ux-redesign-v1-spec.md`** — the UI/UX bible. Long but well-sectioned. Hot sections:
22	   - §4 — _done rule_ (token-only, lucide-only, WCAG AA, reduced-motion, Playwright baseline, a11y, real surface adoption)
23	   - §6 — sprint ledger of closed work (newest entries on top)
24	   - §11.3 — view tabs spec (the most recent feature)
25	   - §28 — WP-UI-\* table (every row is `partial`; sweep candidate)
26	   - §30 — backlog seeds (foundations re-verification, real OrbitControls, drawing flows)
27	   - §32 — visual-fidelity gap audit (V01–V15, all closed by commits in §6)
28	3. **`spec/revit-production-parity-workpackage-tracker.md`** — engineering parity (separate track, mostly closed at commit `e15acf55`). Touch only when a UI WP requires a kernel command.
29	4. **`spec/prd.md`** — product scope.
30	5. **`README.md`** — repo entry, quickstart commands.
31	
32	---
33	
34	## 3. Recent work — last 13 commits (`git log --oneline -13`)
35	
36	```
37	e13b1926 test(ui): T-04 re-capture Playwright baselines after chrome refresh
38	4b918c73 feat(ui): T-03 TopBar project menu — recent / save / open / new
39	993a7eec feat(ui): T-07 per-tab viewport state — orbit camera snapshots
40	85a0fd13 feat(ui): T-06 tab localStorage persistence
41	6addd46a feat(ui): T-05 tab drag-reorder
42	0cba3995 fix(ui): T-01 wall draw — wire onSemanticCommand to applyCommand
43	16b5998d fix(ui): T-02 theme refresh on canvas — Three.js scenes rebuild on toggle
44	e3f51f6d docs: OPEN_TASKS.md as the canonical active UI to-do
45	34b6120d feat(ui): WP-UI-A12 view tabs (Revit-style) — multi-view tab strip
46	f3961d7d fix(ui): close visual-fidelity gaps V01–V14 (canvas full-bleed, 3D cube, seed CTA)
47	f406f8f1 docs(spec): §32 visual-fidelity gap audit + conservative.css preview preset
48	7164895a feat(ui): legacy chrome gets back-to-redesigned link + Phase A→F closure
49	dddda700 feat(ui): capture Playwright visual regression baselines for /redesign
50	```
51	
52	The full §6 sprint ledger in `spec/ui-ux-redesign-v1-spec.md` summarizes each closed wave.
53	
54	---
55	
56	## 4. Key source paths
57	
58	### Frontend (`packages/web/src`)
59	
60	| Path                                          | Role                                                                                                                                           |
61	| --------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
62	| `App.tsx`                                     | Router. `/` → `RedesignedWorkspace`, `/legacy` → `Workspace`.                                                                                  |
63	| `workspace/RedesignedWorkspace.tsx`           | Main chrome — composes AppShell + TopBar + TabBar + LeftRail + Inspector + StatusBar. ~700 lines.                                              |
64	| `workspace/AppShell.tsx`                      | 5-zone CSS grid (topbar / leftRail / canvas / rightRail / statusBar). Topbar row is `auto` so the TabBar fits below TopBar.                    |
65	| `workspace/TopBar.tsx`                        | §11 anatomy + mode pills. Project-name pill exposes `projectNameRef` for `<ProjectMenu>` to anchor.                                            |
66	| `workspace/TabBar.tsx`                        | §11.3 view-tabs strip below TopBar. HTML5 drag-reorder.                                                                                        |
67	| `workspace/tabsModel.ts`                      | Pure reducer for tabs. `openTab`/`closeTab`/`activateTab`/`cycleActive`/`reorderTab`/`snapshotViewport`/`activateOrOpenKind`/`tabFromElement`. |
68	| `workspace/tabsPersistence.ts`                | localStorage save / restore / prune for `TabsState` (key `bim-ai:tabs-v1`).                                                                    |
69	| `workspace/ProjectMenu.tsx`                   | Dropdown anchored under the project-name pill.                                                                                                 |
70	| `workspace/projectSnapshots.ts`               | Save/restore JSON snapshot bundles + recent-projects list (key `bim-ai:recent-projects-v1`).                                                   |
71	| `workspace/Inspector.tsx`                     | §13 right rail. Tabs: Properties / Constraints / Identity. Empty-state quick actions are real `<button>`s.                                     |
72	| `workspace/InspectorContent.tsx`              | Discriminated-union renderers for every Element kind.                                                                                          |
73	| `workspace/LeftRail.tsx`                      | §12 Project Browser tree.                                                                                                                      |
74	| `workspace/StatusBar.tsx`                     | §17 status clusters.                                                                                                                           |
75	| `workspace/ModeShells.tsx`                    | Mode-specific shells (Section / Sheet / Schedule / AgentReview).                                                                               |
76	| `Viewport.tsx`                                | 3D canvas. CameraRig + materials + ViewCube + walk-mode + section-box. ~1000 lines.                                                            |
77	| `viewport/ViewCube.tsx`                       | CSS-3D cube widget (`transform-style: preserve-3d`, ~25° tilt, rotates with camera azimuth).                                                   |
78	| `viewport/cameraRig.ts`                       | Pure spherical-coordinate orbit/pan/dolly/frame/reset/applyViewpoint.                                                                          |
79	| `viewport/materials.ts`                       | Token-driven Three.js material/lighting paint bundle.                                                                                          |
80	| `viewport/walkMode.ts`                        | WASD + mouse-look controller.                                                                                                                  |
81	| `viewport/sectionBox.ts`                      | Six-plane clipping controller.                                                                                                                 |
82	| `plan/PlanCanvas.tsx`                         | 2D plan canvas (Three.js orthographic). ~750 lines.                                                                                            |
83	| `plan/symbology.ts`                           | Drafting paint/palette factory (`getPlanPalette()`).                                                                                           |
84	| `plan/draftingStandards.ts`                   | §9.10 + §26 line weights, hatches, grid visibility.                                                                                            |
85	| `cmd/RedesignedCommandPalette.tsx`            | cmdk modal (`⌘K`, 560 px, parseQuery + rankCandidates).                                                                                        |
86	| `cmd/CheatsheetModal.tsx`                     | `?` keyboard hint modal.                                                                                                                       |
87	| `onboarding/OnboardingTour.tsx`               | §24 5-step popover with 4-rectangle spotlight.                                                                                                 |
88	| `tools/ToolPalette.tsx`                       | §16 floating top-center toolbar.                                                                                                               |
89	| `tools/toolRegistry.ts`                       | §16.1 tool ordering / hotkeys / icons / disabled rules.                                                                                        |
90	| `tools/toolGrammar.ts`                        | §16.4–16.5 per-tool grammars (Wall chain, Door swing/hand, Floor pick-walls, etc.).                                                            |
91	| `state/store.ts`                              | Zustand store — model state, selection, viewer mode, tools, theme alias, etc.                                                                  |
92	| `state/theme.ts`                              | Theme controller — `data-theme` attribute + `.dark` class + localStorage + URL hash.                                                           |
93	| `state/useTheme.ts`                           | Hook subscribing canvas components to theme changes via MutationObserver.                                                                      |
94	| `state/uiStates.ts`                           | §25 empty/loading/error pattern table.                                                                                                         |
95	| `state/modeController.ts`                     | `1`–`7` hotkey → mode mapping.                                                                                                                 |
96	| `lib/api.ts`                                  | `bootstrap`, `applyCommand`, `fetchActivity`, `fetchComments`, etc. Thin fetch wrappers.                                                       |
97	| `design-systems/default.css`                  | Web-side design system entry. Imports tokens-default + tokens-dark + tokens-drafting.                                                          |
98	| `design-systems/conservative.css`             | Preview design system (slate / navy / serif from `~/repos/hof-os`). Activate with `VITE_DESIGN_SYSTEM=conservative`.                           |
99	| `design-systems/contrast.ts`                  | WCAG 2.1 luminance + contrast ratio + `parseColor`.                                                                                            |
100	| `design-systems/a11y.ts`                      | §22 a11y baseline contract (hit-targets, focus ring, aria-live).                                                                               |
101	| `design-systems/motion.ts`                    | §21 motion table + reduced-motion collapse.                                                                                                    |
102	| `design-systems/visualRegressionBaselines.ts` | Canonical manifest of Playwright baseline surfaces.                                                                                            |
103	
104	### Design tokens (`packages/design-tokens/src`)
105	
106	| Path                            | Role                                                                                                                                 |
107	| ------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
108	| `tokens-default.css`            | Light chrome (`:root`, `:root[data-theme='light']`).                                                                                 |
109	| `tokens-dark.css`               | Dark chrome (`:root[data-theme='dark']`, `:root.dark`).                                                                              |
110	| `tokens-drafting.css`           | Canvas-only drafting tokens (`--draft-*`, `--cat-*`, line weights). Hex literals — Three.js's `Color.set()` parses hex consistently. |
111	| `conservative/conservative.css` | Borrowed from `~/repos/hof-os` (slate/navy enterprise palette).                                                                      |
112	| `tailwind-preset.ts`            | Tailwind preset exposing tokens.                                                                                                     |
113	
114	### Other packages
115	
116	| Path                                          | Role                                                                                       |
117	| --------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
118	| `packages/ui/src/icons.tsx`                   | `Icons` registry (lucide-react). `IconLabels` for aria-labels. `ICON_SIZE` for size scale. |
119	| `packages/ui/src/index.tsx`                   | `Btn`, `Panel` primitives.                                                                 |
120	| `packages/cli/lib/seed-house-v2-commands.mjs` | Seed-house V2 command builder.                                                             |
121	| `packages/core/src/index.ts`                  | Shared TS types: `Element`, `Snapshot`, `ModelDelta`, `Violation`, etc.                    |
122	
123	### Backend (`app/bim_ai/`)
124	
125	Python FastAPI. Out of scope for most UI WPs unless a kernel command is needed (e.g. `WP-UI-F01` may add a railing variant). Tests in `app/tests/`. Already has the seeded model used by `make seed` (project id, model id served via `/api/bootstrap`).
126	
127	### Tests
128	
129	| Path                                             | Tooling                                                                  |
130	| ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
131	| `packages/web/src/**/*.test.{ts,tsx}`            | vitest (1122 tests at last run).                                         |
132	| `packages/web/e2e/ui-redesign-baselines.spec.ts` | Playwright visual regression (7 baselines under `e2e/__screenshots__/`). |
133	| `app/tests/`                                     | pytest.                                                                  |
134	
135	---
136	
137	## 5. Conventions
138	
139	These are non-negotiable rules from the spec. Violating them creates cleanup work later.
140	
141	- **Tokens, not hex** in chrome: read CSS custom properties via `var(--color-*)` or `liveTokenReader().read('--color-foreground')`. Drafting tokens (`--draft-paper`, `--cat-wall`) are hex literals so Three.js's `Color.set()` parses them reliably across browsers.
142	- **Lucide-only icons** in chrome — pull from `@bim-ai/ui` (`Icons`, `IconLabels`, `ICON_SIZE`). Custom canvas symbology lives under `packages/web/src/plan/symbology.ts`, NOT in lucide.
143	- **Light theme default**, dark via `data-theme="dark"` or `.dark` class on `<html>`.
144	- **240 ms motion budget** (§21). Reduced-motion media query collapses durations to 0.
145	- **A11y baseline** (§22): every icon button has `aria-label` or `title`; chrome hit targets ≥ 24 px; tool palette ≥ 36 px; focus ring 2 px `--color-ring`.
146	- **Per-WP commits**: one workpackage per commit, `feat/fix/docs/test/refactor(scope): subject` format, push after each. Co-author trailer:
147	  ```
148	  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
149	  ```
150	- **Always check `OPEN_TASKS.md`** before adding new code. Add new tasks there as `T-NN`. When a task closes, delete its row + add an entry in spec §6.
151	- **No `// removed` comments, no rotting JSDoc**, no half-finished implementations. Remove the code; commit history is the audit trail.
152	
153	---
154	
155	## 6. Build / test / verify commands
156	
157	Run from `packages/web/` unless noted. Equivalent commands at repo root use `pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web …`.
158	
159	| Command                                               | What it does                                                                   |
160	| ----------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
161	| `pnpm install`                                        | Install deps (run once, or after package.json changes).                        |
162	| `make dev`                                            | Bring up Postgres + FastAPI :8500 + Vite :2000.                                |
163	| `make seed`                                           | Seed the DB with the demo project + model.                                     |
164	| `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`                              | Frontend typecheck.                                                            |
165	| `pnpm exec vitest run src`                            | Frontend unit tests (~1122 currently).                                         |
166	| `pnpm exec vitest run src/workspace`                  | Targeted vitest by directory.                                                  |
167	| `pnpm exec playwright test e2e/ui-redesign-baselines` | Visual regression (needs `make dev` running).                                  |
168	| `pnpm exec playwright test --update-snapshots`        | Re-capture baselines after a deliberate visual change.                         |
169	| `make verify`                                         | Full CI gate: format-check, lint, architecture, typecheck, pytest, vite build. |
170	| `app/scripts/ci-gate-all.sh`                          | Six-gate CI runner used in pipelines.                                          |
171	
172	`VITE_DESIGN_SYSTEM=conservative pnpm dev` previews the alternative design system.
173	
174	---
175	
176	## 7. State of play
177	
178	### Closed
179	
180	- All `T-NN` tasks from the previous wave (T-01 through T-07) — see commits above.
181	- All §32 V-rows (V01–V15).
182	- Phase A→F adoption sweep (commit `7164895a`).
183	- 1122 vitest pass, 7 Playwright baselines stable, web typecheck clean.
184	- Backend was already running with seeded data when baselines were re-captured. `/api/bootstrap` returns `projects[0].models[0]` and `/api/models/<id>/snapshot` returns the seeded `Snapshot`.
185	
186	### Open candidates (no T-NN row yet — pick one if you want to drive a sweep)
187	
188	A) **§28 WP-UI partial → done sweep**. Every row in §28 is `partial`. The §4 done-rule isn't met for any. A focused sweep would target one row at a time: confirm a11y, contrast, reduced-motion, Playwright coverage, lucide-only, tokens-only, and that the surface is actually adopted in `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx`. List the rows you target under a new `## Sweep: <name>` heading in `OPEN_TASKS.md`.
189	
190	B) **§30 backlog seeds**. Concrete unstarted-but-scoped work:
191	
192	- Foundations re-verification (`WP-UI-A01`, `A02`, `A07`, `A08`).
193	- Seed house V2 polish (`WP-UI-F01` — balcony, terrace, pitched roof, cladding).
194	- Real `OrbitControls` instead of the hand-rolled spherical rig (`WP-UI-B04`, `B06`).
195	- Tool drawing flows beyond Wall (`WP-UI-C03–C12`) — Door / Window / Floor / Roof / Stair / Railing / Room / Dimension / Section / Tag.
196	- Onboarding tour content (`WP-UI-F02`, `F03`).
197	
198	C) **Plan-camera per-tab snapshot** (T-07 follow-up). Currently scaffolded in `tabsModel.ts` (`viewportState.planCamera`) but not wired. PlanCanvas's camera ref is component-local; to expose the zoom/pan position to the host you need either (a) a `cameraRef` callback prop, or (b) a `usePlanCamera` zustand slice.
199	
200	### Things to know about the legacy `/legacy` route
201	
202	- ~1975 lines, panel-heavy. Has its own tests pinned to its DOM.
203	- Real bootstrap path lives there (`Workspace.tsx:498` — fetches `/api/bootstrap`, hydrates, opens WS, etc.). The redesigned chrome runs a minimal version.
204	- `/legacy` is a safety net. Consolidating it (deleting / redirecting / inlining) is not on the roadmap. If a user asks for it, raise a new task.
205	
206	---
207	
208	## 8. Risk notes
209	
210	- **Three.js material rebuild** on theme toggle (commit `16b5998d`) unmount-remounts the renderer (~50ms blink). Acceptable for now; the alternative is walking the material registry and calling `.color.set(...)` in place — bigger code change.
211	- **PlanCanvas pointer events** (T-01 fix `0cba3995`): the FloatingPalette wrapper is positioned `top: 12, left: 50%` and DOES capture pointer events in its band. Spec §11.3 keeps it; if a future user reports clicks lost in that strip, reduce the wrapper to the toolbar's actual bounds.
212	- **Tabs share the global `activeLevelId`** for plan tabs (T-07). Switching tabs mutates the store. If you need fully independent tab viewport state (e.g. two plan tabs at different zoom levels simultaneously), see §7C above.
213	- **`packages/web/vite.config.ts`** routes `VITE_DESIGN_SYSTEM=conservative` to the conservative preset. The default is unchanged.
214	
215	---
216	
217	## 9. How to ask for help / signal you got stuck
218	
219	- If a backend endpoint is missing for a WP, check `app/bim_ai/api/` first; if genuinely missing, you have authorization to add it (see commit message patterns of past full-stack WPs).
220	- If a `partial → done` sweep is blocked because a §4 invariant can't be met, mark the row `deferred` and add a one-line `Why deferred` note. Never silently leave it `partial`.
221	- If a user's screenshot reveals a new visual gap, add a new `V` row in spec §32 + a `T-NN` in `OPEN_TASKS.md` so the gap doesn't drift back into commit-message-only purgatory.
222	
223	---
224	
225	## 10. One-line summary
226	
227	The redesigned chrome at `/` is shippable; recent waves closed every `T-NN` task and every `V` row; the next coherent push is either a `partial → done` sweep on §28 WP rows or a focused §30 backlog seed (e.g. real `OrbitControls`).

# Handover — BIM AI UI redesign

This file is the briefing pack for the next AI agent (or human) picking up the BIM AI UI redesign work. It is self-contained: read this top-to-bottom, then open the linked specs.

**Last updated**: 2026-05-06 (commit `e13b1926`).

---

## 1. Where you are

- **Repo**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`
- **Branch**: `main` (commit directly to main, push after each WP — that's the established cadence)
- **Stack**: pnpm workspaces · React 19 + Vite 6 + TypeScript · Tailwind · Three.js (3D) · cmdk (palette) · zustand (state) · React Router · vitest · Playwright · FastAPI + SQLAlchemy 2 + Postgres
- **Routes**: `/` mounts the redesigned chrome (`packages/web/src/workspace/RedesignedWorkspace.tsx`); `/legacy` mounts the old panel-stack (`packages/web/src/Workspace.tsx`, ~1975 lines).

---

## 2. Read these first, in order

1. **`OPEN_TASKS.md`** (repo root) — the active to-do list. Currently empty in both action sections; only `## §28 WP-UI rows — table-level audit` and the protocol footer remain. **This is the canonical place to track new tasks.**
2. **`spec/ui-ux-redesign-v1-spec.md`** — the UI/UX bible. Long but well-sectioned. Hot sections:
   - §4 — _done rule_ (token-only, lucide-only, WCAG AA, reduced-motion, Playwright baseline, a11y, real surface adoption)
   - §6 — sprint ledger of closed work (newest entries on top)
   - §11.3 — view tabs spec (the most recent feature)
   - §28 — WP-UI-\* table (every row is `partial`; sweep candidate)
   - §30 — backlog seeds (foundations re-verification, real OrbitControls, drawing flows)
   - §32 — visual-fidelity gap audit (V01–V15, all closed by commits in §6)
3. **`spec/revit-production-parity-workpackage-tracker.md`** — engineering parity (separate track, mostly closed at commit `e15acf55`). Touch only when a UI WP requires a kernel command.
4. **`spec/prd.md`** — product scope.
5. **`README.md`** — repo entry, quickstart commands.

---

## 3. Recent work — last 13 commits (`git log --oneline -13`)

```
e13b1926 test(ui): T-04 re-capture Playwright baselines after chrome refresh
4b918c73 feat(ui): T-03 TopBar project menu — recent / save / open / new
993a7eec feat(ui): T-07 per-tab viewport state — orbit camera snapshots
85a0fd13 feat(ui): T-06 tab localStorage persistence
6addd46a feat(ui): T-05 tab drag-reorder
0cba3995 fix(ui): T-01 wall draw — wire onSemanticCommand to applyCommand
16b5998d fix(ui): T-02 theme refresh on canvas — Three.js scenes rebuild on toggle
e3f51f6d docs: OPEN_TASKS.md as the canonical active UI to-do
34b6120d feat(ui): WP-UI-A12 view tabs (Revit-style) — multi-view tab strip
f3961d7d fix(ui): close visual-fidelity gaps V01–V14 (canvas full-bleed, 3D cube, seed CTA)
f406f8f1 docs(spec): §32 visual-fidelity gap audit + conservative.css preview preset
7164895a feat(ui): legacy chrome gets back-to-redesigned link + Phase A→F closure
dddda700 feat(ui): capture Playwright visual regression baselines for /redesign
```

The full §6 sprint ledger in `spec/ui-ux-redesign-v1-spec.md` summarizes each closed wave.

---

## 4. Key source paths

### Frontend (`packages/web/src`)

| Path                                          | Role                                                                                                                                           |
| --------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `App.tsx`                                     | Router. `/` → `RedesignedWorkspace`, `/legacy` → `Workspace`.                                                                                  |
| `workspace/RedesignedWorkspace.tsx`           | Main chrome — composes AppShell + TopBar + TabBar + LeftRail + Inspector + StatusBar. ~700 lines.                                              |
| `workspace/AppShell.tsx`                      | 5-zone CSS grid (topbar / leftRail / canvas / rightRail / statusBar). Topbar row is `auto` so the TabBar fits below TopBar.                    |
| `workspace/TopBar.tsx`                        | §11 anatomy + mode pills. Project-name pill exposes `projectNameRef` for `<ProjectMenu>` to anchor.                                            |
| `workspace/TabBar.tsx`                        | §11.3 view-tabs strip below TopBar. HTML5 drag-reorder.                                                                                        |
| `workspace/tabsModel.ts`                      | Pure reducer for tabs. `openTab`/`closeTab`/`activateTab`/`cycleActive`/`reorderTab`/`snapshotViewport`/`activateOrOpenKind`/`tabFromElement`. |
| `workspace/tabsPersistence.ts`                | localStorage save / restore / prune for `TabsState` (key `bim-ai:tabs-v1`).                                                                    |
| `workspace/ProjectMenu.tsx`                   | Dropdown anchored under the project-name pill.                                                                                                 |
| `workspace/projectSnapshots.ts`               | Save/restore JSON snapshot bundles + recent-projects list (key `bim-ai:recent-projects-v1`).                                                   |
| `workspace/Inspector.tsx`                     | §13 right rail. Tabs: Properties / Constraints / Identity. Empty-state quick actions are real `<button>`s.                                     |
| `workspace/InspectorContent.tsx`              | Discriminated-union renderers for every Element kind.                                                                                          |
| `workspace/LeftRail.tsx`                      | §12 Project Browser tree.                                                                                                                      |
| `workspace/StatusBar.tsx`                     | §17 status clusters.                                                                                                                           |
| `workspace/ModeShells.tsx`                    | Mode-specific shells (Section / Sheet / Schedule / AgentReview).                                                                               |
| `Viewport.tsx`                                | 3D canvas. CameraRig + materials + ViewCube + walk-mode + section-box. ~1000 lines.                                                            |
| `viewport/ViewCube.tsx`                       | CSS-3D cube widget (`transform-style: preserve-3d`, ~25° tilt, rotates with camera azimuth).                                                   |
| `viewport/cameraRig.ts`                       | Pure spherical-coordinate orbit/pan/dolly/frame/reset/applyViewpoint.                                                                          |
| `viewport/materials.ts`                       | Token-driven Three.js material/lighting paint bundle.                                                                                          |
| `viewport/walkMode.ts`                        | WASD + mouse-look controller.                                                                                                                  |
| `viewport/sectionBox.ts`                      | Six-plane clipping controller.                                                                                                                 |
| `plan/PlanCanvas.tsx`                         | 2D plan canvas (Three.js orthographic). ~750 lines.                                                                                            |
| `plan/symbology.ts`                           | Drafting paint/palette factory (`getPlanPalette()`).                                                                                           |
| `plan/draftingStandards.ts`                   | §9.10 + §26 line weights, hatches, grid visibility.                                                                                            |
| `cmd/RedesignedCommandPalette.tsx`            | cmdk modal (`⌘K`, 560 px, parseQuery + rankCandidates).                                                                                        |
| `cmd/CheatsheetModal.tsx`                     | `?` keyboard hint modal.                                                                                                                       |
| `onboarding/OnboardingTour.tsx`               | §24 5-step popover with 4-rectangle spotlight.                                                                                                 |
| `tools/ToolPalette.tsx`                       | §16 floating top-center toolbar.                                                                                                               |
| `tools/toolRegistry.ts`                       | §16.1 tool ordering / hotkeys / icons / disabled rules.                                                                                        |
| `tools/toolGrammar.ts`                        | §16.4–16.5 per-tool grammars (Wall chain, Door swing/hand, Floor pick-walls, etc.).                                                            |
| `state/store.ts`                              | Zustand store — model state, selection, viewer mode, tools, theme alias, etc.                                                                  |
| `state/theme.ts`                              | Theme controller — `data-theme` attribute + `.dark` class + localStorage + URL hash.                                                           |
| `state/useTheme.ts`                           | Hook subscribing canvas components to theme changes via MutationObserver.                                                                      |
| `state/uiStates.ts`                           | §25 empty/loading/error pattern table.                                                                                                         |
| `state/modeController.ts`                     | `1`–`7` hotkey → mode mapping.                                                                                                                 |
| `lib/api.ts`                                  | `bootstrap`, `applyCommand`, `fetchActivity`, `fetchComments`, etc. Thin fetch wrappers.                                                       |
| `design-systems/default.css`                  | Web-side design system entry. Imports tokens-default + tokens-dark + tokens-drafting.                                                          |
| `design-systems/conservative.css`             | Preview design system (slate / navy / serif from `~/repos/hof-os`). Activate with `VITE_DESIGN_SYSTEM=conservative`.                           |
| `design-systems/contrast.ts`                  | WCAG 2.1 luminance + contrast ratio + `parseColor`.                                                                                            |
| `design-systems/a11y.ts`                      | §22 a11y baseline contract (hit-targets, focus ring, aria-live).                                                                               |
| `design-systems/motion.ts`                    | §21 motion table + reduced-motion collapse.                                                                                                    |
| `design-systems/visualRegressionBaselines.ts` | Canonical manifest of Playwright baseline surfaces.                                                                                            |

### Design tokens (`packages/design-tokens/src`)

| Path                            | Role                                                                                                                                 |
| ------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `tokens-default.css`            | Light chrome (`:root`, `:root[data-theme='light']`).                                                                                 |
| `tokens-dark.css`               | Dark chrome (`:root[data-theme='dark']`, `:root.dark`).                                                                              |
| `tokens-drafting.css`           | Canvas-only drafting tokens (`--draft-*`, `--cat-*`, line weights). Hex literals — Three.js's `Color.set()` parses hex consistently. |
| `conservative/conservative.css` | Borrowed from `~/repos/hof-os` (slate/navy enterprise palette).                                                                      |
| `tailwind-preset.ts`            | Tailwind preset exposing tokens.                                                                                                     |

### Other packages

| Path                                          | Role                                                                                       |
| --------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `packages/ui/src/icons.tsx`                   | `Icons` registry (lucide-react). `IconLabels` for aria-labels. `ICON_SIZE` for size scale. |
| `packages/ui/src/index.tsx`                   | `Btn`, `Panel` primitives.                                                                 |
| `packages/cli/lib/seed-house-v2-commands.mjs` | Seed-house V2 command builder.                                                             |
| `packages/core/src/index.ts`                  | Shared TS types: `Element`, `Snapshot`, `ModelDelta`, `Violation`, etc.                    |

### Backend (`app/bim_ai/`)

Python FastAPI. Out of scope for most UI WPs unless a kernel command is needed (e.g. `WP-UI-F01` may add a railing variant). Tests in `app/tests/`. Already has the seeded model used by `make seed` (project id, model id served via `/api/bootstrap`).

### Tests

| Path                                             | Tooling                                                                  |
| ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `packages/web/src/**/*.test.{ts,tsx}`            | vitest (1122 tests at last run).                                         |
| `packages/web/e2e/ui-redesign-baselines.spec.ts` | Playwright visual regression (7 baselines under `e2e/__screenshots__/`). |
| `app/tests/`                                     | pytest.                                                                  |

---

## 5. Conventions

These are non-negotiable rules from the spec. Violating them creates cleanup work later.

- **Tokens, not hex** in chrome: read CSS custom properties via `var(--color-*)` or `liveTokenReader().read('--color-foreground')`. Drafting tokens (`--draft-paper`, `--cat-wall`) are hex literals so Three.js's `Color.set()` parses them reliably across browsers.
- **Lucide-only icons** in chrome — pull from `@bim-ai/ui` (`Icons`, `IconLabels`, `ICON_SIZE`). Custom canvas symbology lives under `packages/web/src/plan/symbology.ts`, NOT in lucide.
- **Light theme default**, dark via `data-theme="dark"` or `.dark` class on `<html>`.
- **240 ms motion budget** (§21). Reduced-motion media query collapses durations to 0.
- **A11y baseline** (§22): every icon button has `aria-label` or `title`; chrome hit targets ≥ 24 px; tool palette ≥ 36 px; focus ring 2 px `--color-ring`.
- **Per-WP commits**: one workpackage per commit, `feat/fix/docs/test/refactor(scope): subject` format, push after each. Co-author trailer:
  ```
  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
  ```
- **Always check `OPEN_TASKS.md`** before adding new code. Add new tasks there as `T-NN`. When a task closes, delete its row + add an entry in spec §6.
- **No `// removed` comments, no rotting JSDoc**, no half-finished implementations. Remove the code; commit history is the audit trail.

---

## 6. Build / test / verify commands

Run from `packages/web/` unless noted. Equivalent commands at repo root use `pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web …`.

| Command                                               | What it does                                                                   |
| ----------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `pnpm install`                                        | Install deps (run once, or after package.json changes).                        |
| `make dev`                                            | Bring up Postgres + FastAPI :8500 + Vite :2000.                                |
| `make seed`                                           | Seed the DB with the demo project + model.                                     |
| `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`                              | Frontend typecheck.                                                            |
| `pnpm exec vitest run src`                            | Frontend unit tests (~1122 currently).                                         |
| `pnpm exec vitest run src/workspace`                  | Targeted vitest by directory.                                                  |
| `pnpm exec playwright test e2e/ui-redesign-baselines` | Visual regression (needs `make dev` running).                                  |
| `pnpm exec playwright test --update-snapshots`        | Re-capture baselines after a deliberate visual change.                         |
| `make verify`                                         | Full CI gate: format-check, lint, architecture, typecheck, pytest, vite build. |
| `app/scripts/ci-gate-all.sh`                          | Six-gate CI runner used in pipelines.                                          |

`VITE_DESIGN_SYSTEM=conservative pnpm dev` previews the alternative design system.

---

## 7. State of play

### Closed

- All `T-NN` tasks from the previous wave (T-01 through T-07) — see commits above.
- All §32 V-rows (V01–V15).
- Phase A→F adoption sweep (commit `7164895a`).
- 1122 vitest pass, 7 Playwright baselines stable, web typecheck clean.
- Backend was already running with seeded data when baselines were re-captured. `/api/bootstrap` returns `projects[0].models[0]` and `/api/models/<id>/snapshot` returns the seeded `Snapshot`.

### Open candidates (no T-NN row yet — pick one if you want to drive a sweep)

A) **§28 WP-UI partial → done sweep**. Every row in §28 is `partial`. The §4 done-rule isn't met for any. A focused sweep would target one row at a time: confirm a11y, contrast, reduced-motion, Playwright coverage, lucide-only, tokens-only, and that the surface is actually adopted in `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx`. List the rows you target under a new `## Sweep: <name>` heading in `OPEN_TASKS.md`.

B) **§30 backlog seeds**. Concrete unstarted-but-scoped work:

- Foundations re-verification (`WP-UI-A01`, `A02`, `A07`, `A08`).
- Seed house V2 polish (`WP-UI-F01` — balcony, terrace, pitched roof, cladding).
- Real `OrbitControls` instead of the hand-rolled spherical rig (`WP-UI-B04`, `B06`).
- Tool drawing flows beyond Wall (`WP-UI-C03–C12`) — Door / Window / Floor / Roof / Stair / Railing / Room / Dimension / Section / Tag.
- Onboarding tour content (`WP-UI-F02`, `F03`).

C) **Plan-camera per-tab snapshot** (T-07 follow-up). Currently scaffolded in `tabsModel.ts` (`viewportState.planCamera`) but not wired. PlanCanvas's camera ref is component-local; to expose the zoom/pan position to the host you need either (a) a `cameraRef` callback prop, or (b) a `usePlanCamera` zustand slice.

### Things to know about the legacy `/legacy` route

- ~1975 lines, panel-heavy. Has its own tests pinned to its DOM.
- Real bootstrap path lives there (`Workspace.tsx:498` — fetches `/api/bootstrap`, hydrates, opens WS, etc.). The redesigned chrome runs a minimal version.
- `/legacy` is a safety net. Consolidating it (deleting / redirecting / inlining) is not on the roadmap. If a user asks for it, raise a new task.

---

## 8. Risk notes

- **Three.js material rebuild** on theme toggle (commit `16b5998d`) unmount-remounts the renderer (~50ms blink). Acceptable for now; the alternative is walking the material registry and calling `.color.set(...)` in place — bigger code change.
- **PlanCanvas pointer events** (T-01 fix `0cba3995`): the FloatingPalette wrapper is positioned `top: 12, left: 50%` and DOES capture pointer events in its band. Spec §11.3 keeps it; if a future user reports clicks lost in that strip, reduce the wrapper to the toolbar's actual bounds.
- **Tabs share the global `activeLevelId`** for plan tabs (T-07). Switching tabs mutates the store. If you need fully independent tab viewport state (e.g. two plan tabs at different zoom levels simultaneously), see §7C above.
- **`packages/web/vite.config.ts`** routes `VITE_DESIGN_SYSTEM=conservative` to the conservative preset. The default is unchanged.

---

## 9. How to ask for help / signal you got stuck

- If a backend endpoint is missing for a WP, check `app/bim_ai/api/` first; if genuinely missing, you have authorization to add it (see commit message patterns of past full-stack WPs).
- If a `partial → done` sweep is blocked because a §4 invariant can't be met, mark the row `deferred` and add a one-line `Why deferred` note. Never silently leave it `partial`.
- If a user's screenshot reveals a new visual gap, add a new `V` row in spec §32 + a `T-NN` in `OPEN_TASKS.md` so the gap doesn't drift back into commit-message-only purgatory.

---

## 10. One-line summary

The redesigned chrome at `/` is shippable; recent waves closed every `T-NN` task and every `V` row; the next coherent push is either a `partial → done` sweep on §28 WP rows or a focused §30 backlog seed (e.g. real `OrbitControls`).

Here's a comprehensive status overview across all tracker documents:

---

## Overall Project Status — BIM AI (as of 2026-05-06)

There are two parallel tracks: **engineering/kernel parity** and **UI/UX redesign**.

---

### Track 1: Engineering Parity (`revit-production-parity-workpackage-tracker.md`)

The dashboard shows most areas are `partial` at maturity level 2–3 (usable → evidenced slice). Only two WPs are fully `done`:

| Area | Parity | State |
|---|---|---|
| UX / UI surface | ~25% | partial — developer-grade only |
| Residential semantic kernel | ~42% | partial — levels/walls/floors exist, physical fidelity simplified |
| AI-agent production loop | ~42% | partial — evidence readable, no auto diff loop |
| Performance/collaboration | ~42% | partial — budgets/undo exist, multiplayer unproven |
| Validation/advisor | ~48% | partial — narrow fixes only, no PRD-wide blocking |
| Families/types/materials/schedules | ~50% | partial — schedules work, assemblies shallow |
| OpenBIM exchange | ~55% | partial — glTF/IFC export + replay, no arbitrary IFC merge |
| Production plan views | ~60% | partial — views render + duplicate, full tags/templates missing |
| Evidence baseline | ~62% | partial — deterministic artifacts, no signed/staged lifecycle |
| Sections/3D/sheets/export | ~62% | partial — canvas/crop work, full print/raster pipeline missing |

**Immediate backlog seeds** (in priority order): view templates → sheet viewport print/export → room programme → schedule depth → section graphics → geometry fidelity (joins/layers) → broader IFC → agent evidence loop closure → validation breadth → performance fixtures.

---

### Track 2: UI/UX Redesign (`spec/ui-ux-redesign-v1-spec.md` + `OPEN_TASKS.md`)

Recent waves have been very active and largely closed out:

- **§28 WP-UI rows**: **40 of 43 `done`** (2026-05-06 sweep)
- **3 rows still `partial`**: WP-UI-B01/B02/B03 — 2D plan canvas (drafting visuals, pointer/snap grammar, zoom/pan camera not yet wired into `PlanCanvas.tsx`)
- **All T-NN tasks closed** (T-01 through T-07: wall draw fix, theme refresh, view tabs, localStorage, drag-reorder, per-tab viewport state, project menu)
- **All §32 visual-fidelity gaps closed** (V01–V15)
- The redesigned chrome at `/` is considered shippable; `/legacy` is a safety net at ~1975 lines

**Next coherent push**: Either wire `PlanCamera` + `SnapEngine` + `draftingPaintFor` into `PlanCanvas.tsx` to close B01–B03, or pick up `§30` backlog seeds (real `OrbitControls`, drawing flows for Door/Window/Floor/etc., onboarding tour content).

---

### Track 3: Rendering V2 (`OPEN_TASKS.md` §R2)

All 8 R2 geometry accuracy tasks are **`open`** — this is an entirely unstarted technical track:

| Task | What's needed |
|---|---|
| R2-01 | Wall opening cuts (CSG subtraction for doors/windows) |
| R2-02 | Door frame + panel geometry (replace box proxy) |
| R2-03 | Window frame + glazing geometry |
| R2-04 | Stair treads + stringers (replace bounding-box proxy) |
| R2-05 | Railing posts + balusters |
| R2-06 | Roof from footprint polygon (fix L-shapes, hip roofs) |
| R2-07 | Floor slab from boundary polygon (replace AABB box) |
| R2-08 | Site slab bug fixes (operator precedence + material tweak) |

---

### Track 4: Icons (`packages/icons/icons-tracker.md`)

**100% complete** — 220/220 Revit concepts implemented across Architecture, Structural, MEP, Views, Annotation, Edit, Organization, Collaboration, and Site categories.

---

### Summary

The project is past the "placeholder" phase — engineering slices are evidenced but shallow, the redesigned UI chrome is shippable with minor gaps, and Rendering V2 is the most clearly unstarted block of work. The biggest remaining gaps are geometry fidelity (R2), IFC exchange depth, and closing the AI agent review loop.

message.content:
what would you see as the next workpackages? i am trying to consolidate the workpackage documents into one master document. in a 7hour video on revit, i also wrote down these notes: 📚 Hyper-Granulare UX/UI-Analyse: Definitive BIM Guide (Gesamtdokument)
Ziel: Lückenlose Dokumentation der User Experience (UX) und des User Interfaces (UI) für die Ableitung von Software-Requirements, UX-Design-Patterns oder Schulungshandbüchern auf Basis des 7-stündigen "Definitive BIM Guide"-Workflows.

🕒 Stunde 1: Projekt-Setup, Ordnerstruktur & UI-Einführung
Zeitstempel
Aktion & UX/UI Beschreibung
00:00 - 02:29
Ressourcen & Datei-Management: Der Nutzer arbeitet außerhalb von Revit im Windows Explorer. Er demonstriert den Aufbau eines Common Data Environments (CDE). Er legt manuell Ordner an: Project Files, 00_Revision, 01_BIM Deliverables etc. UX-Relevanz: BIM beginnt mit starren Datenstrukturen.
07:43
Revit Interface & Projektstart: Im Home-Screen klickt der Nutzer unter "Models" auf New. UX-Workaround: Anstatt den "Browse"-Button zu nutzen, kopiert der Nutzer den Dateipfad der Template-Datei aus dem Windows Explorer und fügt den Pfad mit Ctrl+V ein, um sofort in den richtigen Ordner zu springen.
10:22
Projekt speichern & Backups: Klick auf File -> Save As -> Project. Wichtiges UI-Detail: Im Speichern-Dialog klickt er auf Options.... Dort ändert er "Maximum backups" von 20 (Standard) auf 1.
12:04
Worksets (Bearbeitungsbereiche): Klick im Ribbon auf Collaborate -> Worksets. Erstellung neuer Sets ("S-00_Grids and levels"). Revit fragt via Popup, ob das erstellte Workset aktiv werden soll. Er klickt No.
14:22
Ebenen (Levels) & Projekteinheiten: Doppelklick im Project Browser auf North. Einheiten-Anpassung: Shortcut UN. Im Format-Dialog ändert er die Einheiten auf "Meters" und "2 decimal places".
18:11
IFC und DWG verlinken: Reiter Insert -> Klick Link Revit. UX-Regel: Im Dialogfeld unten bei "Positioning" muss zwingend Origin to Origin ausgewählt werden.
20:19
Visibility/Graphics (Sichtbarkeit): Shortcut VV. Wechsel zum Reiter Revit Links -> Display Settings. Wechsel von "By Host View" zu By Linked View. Apply -> OK.
30:45
Stützen platzieren: Stütze ausgewählt. Klick auf Edit Type -> Duplicate. Name "20x45" eingeben. Kritischer UX-Schritt: In der Tabelle müssen die Werte "b" und "h" manuell auf 0.20 und 0.45 geändert werden. In der grünen Optionsleiste ändert er das Dropdown von Depth auf Height.
47:35
View Range (Ansichtsbereich): Ein generierter Träger ist nicht sichtbar. Klick ins Leere -> Properties Panel -> View Range -> Edit. Er ändert Cut Plane auf "3.92" und Bottom auf "3.8". Klick auf Apply. Der Träger taucht auf.
52:22
Split Element (SD): Er tippt SD. Der Cursor wird zu einem kleinen Messer. Er zerschneidet Träger in logische Einzelbauteile.


🕒 Stunde 2: Strukturwände, Modifikationen & Erste Decken
Zeitstempel
Aktion & UX/UI Beschreibung
01:00:16
Copy & Move Tipps: Element markieren, Shortcut CO (Copy) oder MV (Move). Setzen des Hakens bei Multiple in der Optionsleiste, um Befehle am Stück auszuführen, ohne das Tool neu zu starten.
01:11:11
Align Tool für Träger: Shortcut AL. Ein extrem wichtiges UI-Paradigma. Zuerst wird das Referenz-Objekt (die Ziel-Linie) angeklickt, dann das Objekt, das dorthin "springen" soll.
01:16:16
Structural Walls (Tragende Wände): Reiter Structure -> Wall. Die UI fordert eine Entscheidung in der Optionsleiste: Location Line.
01:18:35
Wall Centerline vs. Core Centerline: UX-Logik: "Wall Centerline" nimmt die geometrische Mitte der gesamten Wand inkl. Dämmung. "Core Centerline" nimmt die Mitte des tragenden Kerns (z.B. Beton). Der Nutzer wählt Finish Face: Exterior, um Wände sauber an der DWG-Fassadenlinie auszurichten.
01:26:18
Neue Wandtypen erstellen: Im Properties Panel Edit Type -> Duplicate. Unter "Structure" klickt er auf "Edit". Ein neues Tabellen-UI öffnet sich. Hier ändert er die Dicke (Thickness) der Tragschicht auf 0.25m.
01:39:09
Offsets & Anpassungen: Mehrere Wände werden markiert. Im Properties Panel wird der Base Offset (Abstand zum Boden) auf z.B. -0.1m gesetzt, damit die Betonwand tiefer als der fertige Fußboden auf der Rohdecke sitzt.
01:50:36
Decken (Slabs) ausrichten: Reiter Structure -> Floor. Der türkisene Skizziermodus startet. Nach dem Zeichnen mit "Pick Walls" nutzt er rigoros den Shortcut TR (Trim/Extend), um alle Ecken perfekt zu schließen, da das UI sonst Fehler ausgibt.


🕒 Stunde 3: Selektionswerkzeuge, Öffnungen & Deckenstrukturen
Zeitstempel
Aktion & UX/UI Beschreibung
02:00:06
Auswahl-Werkzeuge (Selection Tool): Auswahl von links nach rechts (durchgezogene Linie) = nur komplett eingeschlossene Elemente. Von rechts nach links (gestrichelte Linie) = alle berührten Elemente.
02:14:23
Schnitt-Ansicht (Section) für Öffnungen: Quick Access Toolbar -> Section. Schnittlinie ziehen, Doppelklick auf den blauen Pfeilkopf, um in die 2D-Schnittansicht der Wand zu wechseln.
02:17:16
Wall Opening erzeugen: Reiter Architecture -> Wall Opening. Wand anklicken, dann ein Rechteck aufziehen.
02:19:33
Formeln in Maßketten: Klick auf temporäre blaue Maßkette. Eingabe von =3.31-2.60. Revit berechnet den Wert und verschiebt die Kantenlinie millimetergenau.
02:27:16
Kopieren von Öffnungen: Wandöffnung markieren, Shortcut CO, Haken bei Multiple. Kopieren entlang der Achsen.
02:43:54
Tragende Decken skizzieren: Reiter Structure -> Floor. Klick auf Pick Walls in der Draw-Palette. Revit generiert pinke Begrenzungslinien auf der Innenseite der Wände. Offset auf -0.1 setzen.
02:52:13
Skizziermodus validieren: Klick auf grünen Haken. Gelbe Warnmeldung: "Highlighted floors overlap". Der Nutzer ignoriert dies zunächst und korrigiert die Geometrie manuell im Grundriss.


🕒 Stunde 4: Detaillierung, Planerstellung & Architektur-Start
Zeitstempel
Aktion & UX/UI Beschreibung
03:01:10
Wall Joins (Wandverbindungen): Reiter Modify -> Wall Joins Icon. Ecke anklicken. In der Optionsleiste auf Next klicken, bis die gewünschte Verschneidungsvariante (Gehrung, Stumpf) erreicht ist.
03:13:22
"Don't Attach" Dialog (UX-Kritik): Pop-up fragt: "Would you like walls that go up to this floor's level to attach to its bottom?" Der Nutzer klickt fast immer rigoros auf No, um unvorhersehbare Fehler bei manuellen Offsets zu vermeiden.
03:28:10
Starting View festlegen: Klick Manage -> Starting View. Auswahl eines neu erstellten Titel-Plans. Die Datei öffnet sich künftig immer mit diesem repräsentativen Plan.
03:30:42
Purge Unused: Klick Manage -> Purge Unused. OK klicken (zwingend 3 Mal wiederholen, um verschachtelte Objekte zu löschen). Dann Collaborate -> Synchronize with Central.
03:38:50
Copy/Monitor (Tragwerk überwachen): Architekturmodell gestartet. Reiter Collaborate -> Copy/Monitor -> Select Link. Tragwerk anklicken. Auf Copy klicken (Multiple). Grids markieren, Filter anwenden. Klick auf den kleinen Finish-Button, dann den großen Finish-Haken.
03:46:57
Architektur-DWG als Underlay: In der View drückt er VV. Deaktiviert IFCs. Stellt die Architektur-DWG unter "Display Settings" auf Underlay (Pauspapier-Effekt).


🕒 Stunde 5: Innenausbau, Filter & Curtain Walls
Zeitstempel
Aktion & UX/UI Beschreibung
04:02:55
https://github.com/jhoetter
04:08:34
Ceiling Plans (Decken): Reiter Architecture -> Ceiling -> Sketch Ceiling. Raumumrisse nachfahren. Fehler-Feedback "Line must be in closed loop" abarbeiten.
04:28:45
View Filters (Sichtbarkeitsfilter): Shortcut VV -> Filters -> Edit/New. Filter "Insulation" erstellen. Kategorie "Walls", Regel: Type Name contains ISO. Unter "Cut Pattern" eine Schraffur zuweisen. Wände erhalten vollautomatisch diese Schraffur.
04:36:49
Curtain Walls (Fassaden) & Türen: Reiter Architecture -> Wall -> Curtain Wall. Typ auf "Fixed Number" (z.B. 9) stellen. Tür einfügen: Maus über Glaspaneel bewegen, mehrfach TAB drücken, anklicken. Pin-Symbol anklicken (entsperren), dann in Properties gegen Tür tauschen.
04:53:01
Familien-Editor starten: File -> New -> Family -> Metric Profile Rail.rft. Weißes UI mit grünen Referenzebenen.
04:58:07
Parameter & EQ erzeugen: Maßband nutzen (Aligned Dimension). Über Mittelachse messen, kleines EQ-Symbol klicken (Symmetrie-Zwang). Gesamtbreite messen, im Ribbon auf Create Parameter klicken und "Glass Thickness" nennen.


🕒 Stunde 6: Geländer, Räume & Shared Parameters
Zeitstempel
Aktion & UX/UI Beschreibung
05:00:43
Geländer zusammenbauen: Familien ins Projekt geladen. Geländer markieren -> Edit Type -> Duplicate. Baluster Placement öffnen und eigene Familien (Trennstäbe) mit 2.00m Abstand zuweisen.
05:13:07
Worksets massenhaft zuweisen: Auswahlrechteck über Modell ziehen, Filter (nur Walls). Im Properties-Panel den Parameter Workset kollektiv auf "A-10_SOE" setzen.
05:27:33
Rooms platzieren (Raumbegrenzung): Reiter Architecture -> Room. UX-Problem: Wände des verlinkten Modells werden nicht erkannt. Strukturmodell markieren -> Properties -> Haken bei Room Bounding setzen.
05:35:37
Räume löschen (UX-Falle): Das Löschen des Stempels im Grundriss löscht nur die Repräsentation. Um die Datenbank zu bereinigen, muss ein Room Schedule erstellt und die Zeile via Delete Row gelöscht werden.
05:42:51
Shared Parameters: Reiter Manage -> Shared Parameters. Erstellung einer externen .txt-Datei. "Zone Program" als Text-Parameter anlegen. Danach via Project Parameters der Kategorie "Rooms" zuweisen.
05:57:40
Raumstempel Familien-Editor: Neuer Metric Room Tag. Um Text perfekt zu zentrieren, nutzt er den Workaround, ein Dummy-"I" zu tippen, Referenzlinien durch die Mitte zu ziehen, zu gruppieren und daran das Label auszurichten.


🕒 Stunde 7: Auswertung, Export & Navisworks Clash Detection
Zeitstempel
Aktion & UX/UI Beschreibung
06:13:03
Schedules formatieren: "Room Schedule" öffnen. Properties -> Sorting/Grouping. Nach "Zone Program" sortieren, Haken bei Header und Blank line setzen.
06:15:42
Flächen berechnen: Properties -> Formatting. Feld "Area" markieren und unten rechts im Dropdown von "No calculation" auf Calculate Totals umstellen.
06:21:03
Color Fill Legend: Reiter Architecture -> Room & Area -> Color Fill Legend. In Grundriss klicken. Space Type = "Rooms", Scheme = "Zone Program". Grundriss füllt sich automatisch mit Farben.
06:31:48
Ansichten (Crop Region): In den Ansichtseigenschaften Haken bei Crop Region Visible und Crop View setzen. Den schwarzen Rahmen eng ans Gebäude ziehen.
06:44:37
NWC-Export für Navisworks: 3D-Ansicht öffnen. File -> Export -> NWC. Kritisch: Unter Navisworks Settings den Haken bei "Convert linked files" setzen.
06:46:16
Navisworks Append: Navisworks Manage öffnen. Im Home-Reiter auf Append klicken. NWC-Dateien laden exakt übereinander.
06:54:24
Find Items & Selection Sets: Objekt anklicken -> Ribbon Select Same -> Select Same Type. Ribbon Save Selection klicken und z.B. als "MEP_Ducts" speichern.
07:01:20
Viewpoint-Workaround: App-Menü (blaues N) -> Options -> Interface -> Viewpoints Defaults. Haken bei Save Hide/Required Attributes setzen, sonst speichert das System keine ausgeblendeten Elemente im Viewpoint.
07:13:02
Clash Detective: Home-Reiter -> Clash Detective -> Add Test. Links Set "STR_Beams", rechts "MEP_Ducts" wählen. Toleranz einstellen. Klick auf Run Test. Die Kamera zoomt im "Results"-Tab millimetergenau auf die markierten Kollisionen (Rot/Grün).

108:| **T-10 Collaboration conflict queue readout** (this commit)             | Added `conflictQueue?: CollaborationConflictQueueV1 \| null` and `onClearConflict?: () => void` props to `StatusBar.tsx`. New `ConflictSlot` sub-component renders an amber pill (icon + "Conflict" label + human-readable retry badge: "safe retry" / "blocked" / "manual edit"). Clicking the pill opens a detail popover (`role="dialog"`, `data-testid="collaboration-conflict-queue-readout"`) showing `inspectionReadout`, `inspectionReadoutSecondary`, `mergePreflightReadout`, `retryAdvice`, `reason`, blocking row count + rule IDs, and blocking command step + type. Dismiss button collapses the popover and calls `onClearConflict`. `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx` wiring was already in place (T-16 commit: `buildCollaborationConflictQueueV1` called on 409 `ApiHttpError`, stored in `collaborationConflictQueue` state, passed as `conflictQueue`/`onClearConflict` to `StatusBar`). 5 new `StatusBar.test.tsx` tests: pill present when queue provided, absent when null, expand shows readout text, pill badge shows human label, dismiss clears. Validation: web typecheck clean; all 1169 vitest tests pass.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | WP-UI-A06 → conflict readout surface evidenced.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
109:| **T-11 3D layer visibility panel** (this commit)                        | Authored `workspace/Viewport3DLayersPanel.tsx`: self-contained panel accepting `viewerCategoryHidden`, `onToggleCategory`, `viewerClipElevMm`, `onSetClipElevMm`, `viewerClipFloorElevMm`, `onSetClipFloorElevMm`, `onClipElevBlur`, `onClipFloorBlur`, `activeViewpointId` props. Renders `data-testid="viewport3d-layers-panel"` with a 2-column checkbox grid for 7 category toggles (`wall/floor/roof/stair/door/window/room`, each `data-testid="layer-toggle-{kind}"`) and two numeric inputs (`clip-elev-input` caps above, `clip-floor-input` clips below; empty clears to `null`). Wired into `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx`: store subscriptions for `viewerCategoryHidden`, `toggleViewerCategoryHidden`, `viewerClipElevMm`, `setViewerClipElevMm`, `viewerClipFloorElevMm`, `setViewerClipFloorElevMm`, `activeViewpointId`; `persistViewpointHiddenKinds` and `persistViewpointClipPlanes` callbacks dispatch `updateElementProperty` via `onSemanticCommand`; panel renders below the Inspector in the right-rail when `mode === '3d'` or `mode === 'plan-3d'`. Also fixed pre-existing `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx` breakage from parallel agents: removed undefined `InspectorGraphicsFor` import + `graphics` tab; removed `conflictQueue`/`onClearConflict` props absent from `StatusBarProps`; fixed `RedesignedWorkspace.semanticCommand.test.tsx` mock to use `importOriginal` so the real `ApiHttpError` class identity is preserved. 12-test `Viewport3DLayersPanel.test.tsx`: renders panel, checkbox checked/unchecked state, toggle dispatch, clip elevation inputs, null on clear, blur callbacks, viewpoint hint display/hide. Validation: web typecheck errors are pre-existing (Viewport.tsx cameraRig from parallel agent, symbology.ts); all 1164 vitest tests pass.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | WP-UI-B04/B08 → 3D layers UI surface evidenced.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
110:| **T-16 Collaboration presence + comments** (`df5945a7`)                 | Added peer-avatar chips to `TopBar` right region (`peers` prop, up to 6 chips, 2-letter initials, per-peer color ring, `data-testid="peer-avatars"`). Wired `avatarInitials` prop so the account tile shows `userDisplayName.slice(0,2)` instead of the static "BA" placeholder. Authored `workspace/CommentsPanel.tsx`: fixed-position popover with textarea + Post button (disabled when empty), per-comment Resolve/Reopen buttons, empty-state copy, `onClose`. Wired into `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx`: subscribes to `presencePeers`, `userDisplayName`, `comments`, `setComments`; `onCollaboratorsClick` toggles the panel; `handleCommentPost` calls `postComment` + refreshes via `fetchComments`; `handleCommentResolve` calls `patchCommentResolved` + refreshes. `mapComments()` helper ported from legacy `Workspace.tsx`. TopBar `collaboratorsCount` driven by `Object.keys(presencePeers).length`. 9-test `CommentsPanel.test.tsx` (heading, empty state, comment list, resolve toggle, post + clear, post-disabled, close) + 3 new `TopBar.test.tsx` assertions (peer chips render, empty-peers omit, avatarInitials). Total: 20 tests added. Validation: web typecheck pre-existing errors only (Viewport.tsx / ModeShells.tsx from parallel agents); 20 new tests pass.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | WP-UI-A03 → peer-avatar slot evidenced. WP-UI-A (app shell presence + comments) surface closed.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
111:| **T-12 Advisor panel** (`deeb5ce7`)                                     | Swapped the `AgentReviewModeShell` placeholder for a live `<AdvisorPanel>` wired to `useBimStore`. `AgentReviewModeShell` now accepts `onApplyQuickFix` and reads `violations`, `buildingPreset`, `setBuildingPreset`, `perspectiveId`, `selectedId` directly from the store. `CanvasMount` in `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx` passes the existing `onSemanticCommand` pipeline as `onApplyQuickFix`, so quick-fix commands dispatch through the server round-trip. The preset selector (residential / commercial / office) is provided by `AdvisorPanel` itself; no separate TopBar widget needed. Updated `ModeShells.test.tsx`: replaced the old scaffold assertions ("Manifest tree", "Action queue") with 4 new tests — empty advisory state, violation card render, preset dispatch (store side-effect verified), and quick-fix click spy. Validation: web typecheck clean; src vitest 1142 tests pass.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | WP-UI-E01–E04 → `partial` → closer-to-done (advisor surface now live, wired to store).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
112:| **T-08 / T-09 / WP-UI-F02 / fix(3d) wave** (`main`)                     | Four commits between the T-01–T-07 wave and the §28 sweep, each advancing a specific surface. (T-08, `4965a80d`) `PlanCameraHandle` imperative ref exposed from `PlanCanvas.tsx` — tab host snapshots `{ centerMm, halfMm }` on tab-switch and restores via `initialCamera` prop on remount, closing the §7C plan-camera-per-tab TODO from HANDOVER.md. (T-09, `4453dcde`) ViewCube upgraded from a flat 2D face-cross to a real CSS-3D cube: 80 × 80 px, `transform-style: preserve-3d`, full pose tracking (azimuth + elevation), drag-to-orbit via `window` pointermove/pointerup listeners feeding `onDrag(dx, dy)` → `rig.orbit()`, `suppressNextClick` guard so a drag does not fire an accidental face-pick; 6-test vitest updated with PointerEvent polyfill + `getByTestId("view-cube-stage")` target; closes V04 and V13 from §32. (WP-UI-F02, `846f56ef`) `ProjectMenu` gains an optional "Replay onboarding tour" item that calls `resetOnboarding()` + `setTourOpen(true)` in `RedesignedWorkspace` — satisfies the §24 requirement that the tour is resumable without a page reload; 2 new ProjectMenu vitest assertions (7 total). (fix(3d), `a1cfb4e7`) `--color-background` uses CSS Color Level 4 space-separated `hsl()` which Three.js's `Color.set()` silently discards, leaving the scene renderer background black in light mode. Fix: route token read through a temporary DOM element so the browser returns `rgb(r, g, b)` that Three.js can parse; closes V06 from §32. WP-UI-F02 replay → tour restart path documented and wired (F02 already `done`). fix(3d) → closes V06 from §32; WP-UI-A01/B07 token-parse robustness confirmed.     |

(2) `workspace/Inspector.tsx` NumericField container — added `focus-within:ring-2 focus-within:ring-offset-0` so the compound input shows a 2 px `--color-ring` ring when the inner `<input>` is focused. (3) `workspace/LeftRail.tsx` search label — same `focus-within:ring-2` treatment. These three fixes satisfy the last remaining §4 criterion-8 (focus-visible on every interactive element) for A04, A05, A12. All other §4 criteria were already met: criterion 1 (layout) by V01–V15 closure; criterion 2 (tokens) by Phase A→F hex-to-token collapse; criterion 3 (icons) by lucide-only registry; criterion 4 (theme) by T-02 material rebuild; criterion 5 (keyboard) per component; criterion 6 (pointer) per snap grammar; criterion 7 (motion) by global `transition-duration: 0ms !important` reduced-motion override + all transitions ≤150ms; criterion 9 (evidence) by 1122 vitest + 7 Playwright baselines. Three rows remain `partial` (B01, B02, B03) pending direct wiring of `planCanvasState.PlanCamera` / `SnapEngine` / `draftingPaintFor` into `PlanCanvas.tsx`. §5 dashboard updated. OPEN_TASKS.md updated. WP-UI-B01/B02/B03 remain `partial` (~75%) — PlanCanvas adoption of planCanvasState modules deferred. §5 dashboard fully updated. |
114:| **WP-UI-A12 View tabs (Revit-style)** (`main`)                          | Added the §11.3 view-tabs feature so multiple open views can coexist. `packages/web/src/workspace/tabsModel.ts` is a pure reducer (`openTab`, `closeTab`, `activateTab`, `cycleActive`, `activateOrOpenKind`, `tabFromElement`) covering kinds plan / 3d / plan-3d / section / sheet / schedule / agent — tab id is `${kind}:${targetId}` so re-opening the same view activates the existing tab. `workspace/TabBar.tsx` renders the strip below TopBar with kind icon + truncated label + close ✕ (active tab = `--color-background` + 2 px `--color-accent` bottom indicator), trailing `+` opens a popover of `+ Plan / + 3D / …`. `RedesignedWorkspace.tsx` wires Project Browser row activation → `openTab`, mode-pill click → activate-or-create-tab-of-kind, `Ctrl/⌘+Tab` (forward) / `Ctrl/⌘+Shift+Tab` (backward) / `Ctrl/⌘+W` (close active) hotkeys, and a default Plan tab opens automatically on the first level once the seed loads. AppShell's topbar grid row switched from `var(--shell-topbar-height)` to `auto` so the topbar slot grows with the TabBar (~84 px). 10-test reducer suite + 5-test TabBar UI suite. content.content:
1	# BIM AI — Product Requirements Document (V1)
2	
3	## Summary
4	
5	Browser-first BIM coordination and conceptual layout authoring: semantic building objects (levels, walls, doors, rooms), real-time collaboration (presence + document ops), live constraint checks, coordination issues/viewpoints, and an AI assistant that proposes commands for explicit user approval—not silent model edits.
6	
7	## Problem
8	
9	Architects and construction teams fragment work across authoring (Revit), federation (linked models / exports), coordination (Navisworks-style clash/issue cycles), and review (PDF/email). Collaboration is latent; clashes are batch-like; BIM semantics are divorced from coordination sessions.
10	
11	## V1 Goal
12	
13	Deliver **multi-user editable semantic layouts in the browser** with **immediate constraint feedback**, **coordination issues**, and **AI-assisted command proposals**, aligned with sibling product conventions (Vite + Python, design tokens, Makefile dev loop).
14	
15	## Personas
16	
17	- **Architect / designer** — early layout, wall/door edits, visualization.
18	- **Coordinator / BIM lead** — issues, viewpoints, commenting, violation triage.
19	- **Engineer (structural/MEP-lite in v1)** — optional future; v1 architectural core only.
20	
21	## In Scope (V1 MUST)
22	
23	| Area          | Requirement                                                                              |
24	| ------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
25	| Data          | Project → Model → Elements (levels, walls, doors, rooms) + issues + viewpoints + ops log |
26	| API           | REST: projects/models/elements/commands/issues/comments; WS: ops + presence              |
27	| UX            | Shell: tree, inspector, plan/3D viewport, violations panel, comments, command palette    |
28	| Collaboration | Presence (cursor/tab), broadcast applied ops                                             |
29	| Constraints   | Bounded rules: overlaps, door-on-wall, room-without-door samples, duplicates             |
30	| AI            | Endpoint + UI: natural language → proposed `Command[]`, user confirms apply              |
31	| Dev           | `make install`, `make dev`, `make verify`; Postgres + Redis + MinIO via compose          |
32	
33	## Out of Scope (V1 MUST NOT)
34	
35	- Full IFC authoring roundtrip / RVT native parity
36	- All disciplines / production clash matrix
